Postcode District

GU2

Guildford West

Surrey, South East, England

Compare area View summary

GU2 covers the western residential suburbs of Guildford, including the Stoughton and Worplesdon Road areas in Surrey, South East England. It is predominantly a family-oriented residential district with good access to Guildford town centre and the A3.

Avg price £348,000
1yr change ▲ 7.1%
Crime 31% below avg
Schools 2 Outstanding

Property Prices in GU2

Source: HM Land Registry Price Paid Data

The average property price in GU2 is £348,000, which is 7% above the Surrey average of £326,000, reflecting its desirable suburban character and strong local amenities.

Properties in GU2 cost approximately 21% more than the England and Wales national average of £287,000.

House prices in GU2 have risen 7.1% over the past 12 months and 21.4% over five years.

Average Price by Property Type

Type Average Price Sales (12m)
Detached £650,000 38
Semi-detached £395,000 112
Terraced £315,000 98
Flat £218,000 94
All types £348,000 342

5-Year Price History

Crime & Safety in GU2

Source: data.police.uk (rolling 12 months)

GU2 has one of the lowest crime rates in the Guildford area, with 55.8 crimes per 1,000 residents — 31% below the national average. Violent crime and burglary rates are among the lowest in Surrey.

Crime by Type vs National Average

Schools in GU2

Source: DfE Get Information About Schools / Ofsted

GU2 has 8 schools within or near its boundaries, including 2 Outstanding-rated and 5 Good-rated schools. The district is well-served for primary education in particular.

Nearest Schools

School Type Ofsted Distance
Stoughton Infant School Primary Good 0.4 mi
St Peter's Church of England School Primary Outstanding 0.7 mi

Council Tax

Source: Valuation Office Agency

Council tax in GU2 is collected by Guildford Borough Council. The Band D rate is £2,043/year.

Area Summary

GU

GU2 — Guildford West

Area Summary

GU2 offers a quieter, more suburban alternative to GU1 with lower property prices (£348,000), excellent safety credentials (31% below national crime average), and strong school provision. It appeals particularly to families seeking a residential setting with easy access to Guildford's amenities.
